name: Grade 1 Early Learning — Semester 2
description: An 18-week Grade 1 pathway for independence, friendship skills, goal setting, and learning reflection. Learning builds independence through investigation, guided practice, application, and reflection.
learningGoals: Develop first-grade knowledge and skills in independence, friendship skills, goal setting, and learning reflection.
Use speaking, drawing, writing, numbers, models, or movement to explain thinking.
Work independently when ready and ask for help, feedback, or clarification when needed.

lessons.title: Explore independence, friendship skills, goal setting, and learning reflection
lessons.objective: I can observe, discuss, and record independence, friendship skills, goal setting, and learning reflection by completing a first-grade task and explaining one observation, choice, or strategy.

lessons.objective: I can observe, discuss, and record independence, friendship skills, goal setting, and learning reflection by completing a first-grade task and explaining one observation, choice, or strategy.

resources.title: Head Start Early Learning Outcomes Framework
resources.description: Planning reference for this Grade 1 Early Learning pathway.

units.title: Investigate: independence, friendship skills, goal setting, and learning reflection
units.essentialQuestion: What evidence can we gather about independence, friendship skills, goal setting, and learning reflection?
outcomes: I can observe, discuss, and record independence, friendship skills, goal setting, and learning reflection.
I can use accurate words, pictures, numbers, models, writing, or movement to communicate my thinking.

lessons.title: Explore independence, friendship skills, goal setting, and learning reflection
lessons.objective: I can observe, discuss, and record independence, friendship skills, goal setting, and learning reflection by completing a first-grade task and explaining one observation, choice, or strategy.

activities.title: Notice and investigate
activities.learnerAction: The learner uses age-appropriate materials and first-grade vocabulary to observe, discuss, and record independence, friendship skills, goal setting, and learning reflection. Adult supervision is provided whenever the subject involves tools, heat, animals, water, traffic, or online media.

successCriteria: The learner completes a first-grade task connected to independence, friendship skills, goal setting, and learning reflection.
The learner explains an observation, choice, or strategy with appropriate support.
The learner follows safety, care, and collaboration expectations.
